summary.intent: Address round-5 Codex AI code review findings on a Rust color picker component: fix two confirmed bugs (M5PU: alpha aria-valuenow, M5PY: hex input value), evaluate a third (M5Pc: open None→Some transition), add tests, and update the spec.
summary.outcome: Fixed M5PU and M5PY, added 3 round-5 tests (106 total passing), updated and validated the spec, passed coverage and clippy gates, committed and pushed. M5Pc was confirmed a false positive via empirical verification and a rebuttal posted to Codex.

repo: The spec validator parses YAML frontmatter from markdown spec files and requires the `related` field to be a single-line array — multi-line YAML array format (as reflowed by a markdown formatter) breaks it.
The project uses insta snapshot testing; snapshots must be explicitly accepted after intentional attribute changes (e.g., adding `value` to hex input, switching alpha thumb to percent).
A coverage gate and clippy check are required gates before committing.
Codex code review runs in multiple rounds (round-5 here) with P1/P2 severity findings; the review protocol requires pinging @codex on disputed findings rather than silently resolving them.

code.finding: channel_slider_thumb_attrs was not reporting alpha aria-valuenow as a percent (M5PU bug fix).
hex_input_attrs was missing the value attribute entirely (M5PY bug fix).
on_props_changed uses a `was != Some(now)` guard — this correctly fires on None→Some transitions because None != Some(x) is true in Rust, making M5Pc a false positive.

workflow: Codex false positives should be empirically verified (e.g., run a Rust snippet to confirm None != Some(x)) rather than dismissed from memory alone.
False positives should be surfaced to the user and rebutted on the Codex thread with @codex ping rather than resolved inline.
Snapshot diffs should be inspected before accepting to confirm they match only the intended changes.

friction: A markdown formatter reflowed the YAML `related` frontmatter field from single-line to multi-line array format, silently breaking spec validation — required a manual fix back to single-line.
Needed to run an empirical Rust check to confirm None != Some(x) behavior before trusting the M5Pc false-positive assessment.
open_items: M5Pc: rebuttal posted on the Codex thread pinging @codex, intentionally left open pending their response.
